display.purpose: Major page titles and game state announcements only.

headline.purpose: Modal titles, result headers, lobby state headers, and section starts.

title.purpose: Card titles, room names, vault rows, and player result names.

body.purpose: Instructions, stablecoin states, support copy, and readable explanations.

label.purpose: Buttons, badges, form labels, metadata, and compact status text.

shadows.name: structural-shadow
shadows.purpose: Default tactile depth for cards, buttons, modals, toasts, and game controls.

shadows.name: pressed-state
shadows.purpose: Hover or active feedback for tactile controls.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default product UI feedback for state changes.

motion.name: page-enter
motion.purpose: Current light page transition; avoid expanding this into choreography.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Required alternative for gameplay, result, and transition effects.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.purpose: First expansion breakpoint for room, vault, and profile layouts.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action in a state: create, ready, submit, resolve, deposit, or withdraw.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 2px solid var(--border, #422057); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--main, #FDFB51); color: var(--main-foreground, #422057); box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 var(--border, #422057); font: inherit; font-weight: 500; transition: transform 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translate(2px, 2px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, #422057); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Neutral Button
components.description: Secondary action with the same tactile structure but lower priority.
components.css: .ds-btn-neutral { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; min-height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 2px solid var(--border, #422057); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--secondary-background, #fff); color: var(--foreground, #422057); box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 var(--border, #422057); font: inherit; font-weight: 500; transition: transform 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-neutral:hover { transform: translate(2px, 2px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-neutral:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, #422057); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tactile Card
components.description: Default grouped surface for game modes, lobby state, vault rows, and result panels.
components.html:
Match Result

Winner balance is ready in Vault.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--border, #422057); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--background, #FFFEE8); color: var(--foreground, #422057); box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 var(--border, #422057); } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0; font-weight: 800; line-height: 1.1; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Room names, codes, nicknames, and amount-adjacent text entry.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 40px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 2px solid var(--border, #422057); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--secondary-background, #fff); color: var(--foreground, #422057); font: inherit; font-weight: 500; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--foreground, #422057) 62%, transparent); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, #422057); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Compact mode, stake, room, and state labels.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px; width: fit-content; padding: 2px 10px; border: 2px solid var(--border, #422057); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--main, #FDFB51); color: var(--main-foreground, #422057); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; white-space: nowrap; }

narrative.overview: Sensa Hub is a product UI for a mobile-first Celo skill game. It should feel like a pocket arcade machine: direct, physical, playful, and fast. The same surface also carries stake, refund, result, vault, and withdraw states, so every money moment must feel calm and legible.
keyCharacteristics: Lime Yellow primary actions against Dark Purple structural UI.
Tactile neobrutalist surfaces with dark purple borders and hard shadows.
One primary action per state, especially in room, stake, refund, result, vault, and withdraw flows.
Neutral active gameplay surfaces so surrounding UI color does not bias the player.
Trustworthy stablecoin copy that avoids blockchain jargon.

rules.name: The Lime Means Action Rule
rules.body: Lime Yellow is for the next action, selected state, and important game feedback. Do not use it as a background wash across whole screens.

rules.name: The Purple Carries Trust Rule
rules.body: Dark Purple should hold structure: borders, stage areas, nav, wallet states, and serious copy. It keeps the game from feeling like a toy when money is involved.

rules.name: The Game Neutrality Rule
rules.body: During active color gameplay, the app must switch to monochrome surfaces. Lime, purple, green, amber, red, and blue must not surround the player's color judgment area.

rules.name: The State Color Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, red, and blue are semantic. If a color does not explain state or feedback, remove it.

rules.name: The Short Copy Rule
rules.body: Product copy must be short, direct, and task-focused. Long prose belongs only in support, terms, privacy, and onboarding explanations.

rules.name: The No Badge Soup Rule
rules.body: Labels can be badge-like, but every line of UI must not become a badge. Use badges for mode, state, price, online count, and room identity only.

rules.name: The Structural Shadow Rule
rules.body: If a surface has a 2px dark purple border, its shadow must be hard and aligned to the token. Do not add soft 16px+ blur shadows to the same element.

rules.name: The Modal Restraint Rule
rules.body: Modals are for blocking decisions only. Multi-step room or wallet tasks should prefer step views when inline progression is clearer on mobile.

dos: Do design for 360px mobile first, then expand to wider screens.
Do use Lime Yellow (#FDFB51) and Dark Purple (#422057) as the main Sensa combination.
Do keep Dark Purple as the structural border, stage, and trust color.
Do keep Lime Yellow reserved for primary actions, selected states, and meaningful game feedback.
Do keep primary actions visually singular on each state screen.
Do use MiniPay-safe language: Stablecoin, Deposit, Withdraw, Network fee.
Do keep money states calm, explicit, and recoverable.
Do use neutral monochrome surfaces during active color gameplay.
Do replace legacy brand references with Sensa and Celo.
donts: Don't use generic DeFi dashboard patterns: token tables, yield language, protocol jargon, or wallet-first framing.
Don't use casino UI: flashing rewards, fake jackpot hype, manipulative urgency, or cluttered badges.
Don't use generic SaaS polish; the lime-and-purple pairing must serve action, trust, and game structure.
Don't flood whole screens with Lime Yellow. If everything is highlighted, nothing is actionable.
Don't use Gas, crypto, onramp, or offramp in user-facing copy.
Don't present a raw wallet address as the primary user identity.
Don't leave legacy source brand or network references in user-facing UI, metadata, placeholder content, or docs.
Don't combine the Sensa 2px border language with soft decorative glassmorphism or wide ghost-card shadows.
